sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The player moved UP! Now I can see from the image that the player (magenta square) moved up one row. The path blocks (w/g) also moved up. Let me verify by checking the new position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player new position
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Player area after UP ===")
for i in range(33, 42):
    print(f"  {i}: {lines[i][10:25]}")

# The player moved up. Let me track the path blocks too
print("\n=== Path blocks ===")
for i in range(33, 42):
    for j in range(16, 30):
        if lines[i][j] in 'wg':
            print(f"  ({i},{j}): {lines[i][j]}", end="")
    print()
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The player moved UP by about 3 rows (from row 36-41 to row 33-38). The path blocks (w/g) are now at row 33, extending from col 16 to col 28. The path seems to extend from the player toward the right side of the panel.
Looking at the colored blocks on the right:
- R (red): rows 19-22, cols 42-45
- b (blue): rows 25-28, cols 42-45
- N (light green): rows 31-34, cols 42-45
The player is now at rows 33-38. The path at row 33 aligns with the N block (rows 31-34). 
The goal seems to be: move the player to align with a colored block on the right, then extend the path to reach it. The path is a bridge of alternating w/g blocks.
Let me try moving RIGHT to extend the path further and see if it reaches the N block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player is at rows 33-38, path extends from col 16 to 28 on row 33
# N block is at rows 31-34, cols 42-45
# Let me try RIGHT to extend the path further

action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
